text: The system must keep three artifacts in sync: spec markdown files, pytest test suite, and code implementation
The system must support loading environment variables from .env files
The plumb init command must create a .env file in the project directory
The system must support ANTHROPIC_API_KEY configuration through both environment variables and .env files
Tests must be linked to requirements through requirement ID comments using the format # plumb:req-XXXXXXXX
All tests must be organized in the tests/ directory with specific test files for each component
Tests without requirement links must be treated as sync violations
The system must support file ignore patterns through a .plumbignore file
The system must provide a --all command option for the plumb approve command to approve all changes at once
Generated cache and coverage files must be excluded from commits
The system must include a check command as an alias for manual decision scanning
The system must handle conversations that span multiple Claude Code session files by reading and merging them chronologically
The sync command must provide progress indicators with status updates
The workflow must require an explicit sync step after approving decisions
The system must stage sync output before re-committing changes
The system must use a branch-sharded structure for decision logs
The system must provide CLI commands for merging decision logs from different branches
Users must be able to migrate existing decision logs from the old monolithic format to the new branch-sharded layout
The plumb merge-decisions <branch> command must allow users to merge decisions from feature branches back to main
The system must include a find_decision_branch() function to locate which branch file contains a specific decision ID
The WholeFileSpecUpdater must take full spec content and decisions as input and output section_updates and new_sections
The OutlineMerger component must handle structural changes when new sections need to be added or content needs reordering
The system must include helper functions _clean_duckdb_row() and _to_python_native() for DuckDB result type conversion
All LLM-powered functions must be implemented as DSPy programs, not open-ended agents
The system must use the Anthropic Claude SDK as its inference provider
Claude Sonnet 4.6 must serve as the default model for all programs
The system must operate as a git hook and CLI tool without changing how users write code
The pre-commit hook must ensure that every committed state has been reviewed and approved
The system must use Claude Code session data when available and fall back to diff-only analysis when not available
The system must prevent duplicate decisions through a multi-stage pipeline using exact matching, Jaccard similarity, and LLM semantic analysis
Only prescriptive choices that affect system design, behavior, architecture, or data models must be captured as decisions
Process observations, tooling choices, and diagnostic findings must be filtered out from decisions
Spec updates must use a search-and-replace approach with edits containing old_text/new_text pairs
Section updates must use exact header matching first, then fall back to normalized matching
The package must be installable via both pip and uv using the package name plumb-dev
The CLI command must be plumb
The package must include a comprehensive project description sourced from the README.md file
Tests must support comment-based markers using # plumb:req-XXXXXXXX format
Tests must support function name-based linking using test_req_XXXXXXXX_ format
Both linking formats must be supported for backwards compatibility
The system must filter for spec-relevant content during decision extraction
The system must implement semantic similarity checking to handle duplicate or similar decisions
Classification tasks must not generate reasoning traces to maintain optimal performance
LLM-based deduplication must utilize Claude Haiku 4.5 as the designated model
The deduplicate_decisions() function must accept a use_llm parameter that defaults to False
The system must include dependencies: dspy, anthropic, pytest, pytest-cov, gitpython, click, rich, jsonlines
The system must integrate with git hooks to automatically trigger workflows based on repository events
DSPy programs must support program-specific model configuration through a model configuration array in config.json
Pattern parsing logic must be modularized into separate functions for reusability across programs
The programs module must include an extract_outline function that extracts markdown headers from content
The programs module must include token estimation, chunking, and concurrent mapper functionality
The system must use chunked mapping with ThreadPoolExecutor for concurrent processing
The system must support different merge strategies for result combination
Plumb must store all state in a .plumb/ folder at the root of the user's repository
The .plumb/ folder must be committed to version control
The system must track which requirements have been modified and only send dirty requirements to the mapper
The pre-commit hook must validate API access before proceeding with analysis
The hook must exit non-zero and block the commit if API authentication fails
The hook must analyze the staged diff and the Claude Code conversation log
The hook must write pending decisions to branch-specific decision log files
The hook must set the last_extracted_at timestamp
The hook must print a machine-readable JSON summary of pending decisions to stdout and exit non-zero
Claude Code's skill must read the hook output and present decisions to the user one at a time
The system must include explicit safeguards to never approve, reject, or edit decisions on the user's behalf
Users must be able to approve multiple pending decisions efficiently using the --all flag
For rejected decisions, the skill must invoke plumb modify <id>
The commit must only land when there are zero pending decisions
The system must draft commit messages after decision review and include the list of approved decisions
When API authentication fails, the system must raise a custom PlumbAuthError exception
API key validation must be implemented as a separate validate_api_access function
All authentication functionality must have comprehensive test coverage
The system must support environment variable management using .env files with python-dotenv dependency
The system must use gitignore-style patterns for ignore functionality
The post-commit hook must clear the last_extracted_at timestamp after commit completion
The conversation parser must handle Claude Code's type/message schema format
The system must convert tool usage blocks to text format '[tool: Name] description'
The system must include comprehensive documentation for the Plumb skill
The system must implement intelligent deduplication by first running Jaccard similarity filtering
LLM deduplication must be applied as a second pass when 2 or more candidates remain
The deduplication process must prioritize approved and synced decisions over recent ones
The system must use an expanded context window of 200 decisions instead of 50 for duplicate detection
The system must generate complete and runnable tests rather than stub files with TODO comments
Test generation must use increased context limits to accommodate complete test code
The test generator must only run if tests don't already exist
The system must provide read_all_decisions() as the primary API function for accessing decisions across all branches
The review command must pre-compute decision branches to optimize performance
Users must be able to migrate from the monolithic decision file to the sharded layout using the plumb migrate command
The system must implement spec updates using a single LLM call per spec file
The system must make a second call only when new_sections is non-empty
The system must keep structural reasoning for section placement separate from content generation
The system must accept the risk of unintended edits in exchange for performance gains
The legacy decisions path function must be maintained for migration detection purposes
The hook must analyze the staged diff and read Claude Code's native session files directly
The hook must extract prescriptive choices while excluding observations and diagnostics
The hook must implement within-batch similarity deduplication
The hook must use both last_commit datetime and last_extracted_at timestamp as cutoffs
The hook must print a human-readable summary in TTY mode and machine-readable JSON in non-TTY mode
The hook must never exit non-zero due to an internal Plumb error
The plumb init command must check that the current directory is a git repository
The plumb init command must create the .plumb/ directory if it does not exist
The plumb init command must prompt the user interactively for spec and test paths
The plumb init command must validate that provided paths exist and contain appropriate files
The plumb init command must validate pytest test collection
The plumb init command must write .plumb/config.json with the provided paths
The plumb init command must create a .plumbignore file if it does not exist
The plumb init command must install the git pre-commit hook
The plumb init command must install the Claude Code skill locally
The plumb init command must append a Plumb status block to CLAUDE.md
The plumb init command must run plumb parse-spec for initial parsing
The plumb hook command must read .plumb/config.json and exit 0 silently if not found
The plumb hook command must get the current staged diff via git diff --cached
The plumb hook command must detect amends by comparing HEAD commit's parent SHA to last_commit
The plumb hook command must detect broken references and flag unreachable SHAs
The plumb hook command must run the Diff Analysis DSPy program on the staged diff
The plumb hook command must attempt to locate and read the Claude Code conversation log
The plumb hook command must filter out non-spec-relevant decisions before creating Decision records
The plumb hook command must merge and deduplicate decisions across chunks using DuckDB
The plumb hook command must run Question Synthesizer for decisions with no associated question
The plumb hook command must write new decisions with status pending to decisions.jsonl
The plumb hook command must run plumb parse-spec to update requirements cache for modified spec files
The plumb hook command must update last_commit and last_commit_branch when no pending decisions exist
The plumb diff command must preview what Plumb will capture from staged changes without writing to .plumb/
The plumb review command must provide interactive review of pending decisions for terminal use
The plumb review command must accept an optional --branch flag to filter by branch
The plumb review command must display decision details including question, decision, branch, and file references
The plumb review command must prompt users for approve, reject, edit, or skip actions
The plumb review command must run plumb sync after all decisions are resolved
The plumb approve command must approve a single decision by ID and run plumb sync
The plumb reject command must reject a single decision by ID and record the reason
The plumb edit command must replace decision text and run plumb sync
The plumb modify command must modify staged code to satisfy a rejected decision
The plumb modify command must run pytest to verify tests pass after modification
The plumb modify command must only stage modifications if tests pass
The plumb sync command must add early exit check to avoid expensive operations when no unsynced decisions exist
The plumb sync command must run WholeFileSpecUpdater to process all decisions affecting each spec file together
The plumb sync command must run OutlineMerger when new_sections is non-empty
The plumb sync command must run Test Generator to generate organized tests for uncovered requirements
The plumb sync command must write generated tests to test_generated.py
The plumb sync command must run plumb parse-spec to re-cache requirements
The plumb sync command must set synced_at on each processed decision
The plumb parse-spec command must parse all spec markdown files into explicit requirements
The plumb parse-spec command must assign each requirement a stable ID based on content hash
The plumb parse-spec command must write results to .plumb/requirements.json
The plumb parse-spec command must use regex patterns to extract requirement IDs from test files
The plumb parse-spec command must process requirements through CodeCoverageMapper using chunked mapping
The plumb coverage command must report coverage across code, spec-to-test, and spec-to-code dimensions
The plumb coverage command must run pytest --cov and parse output for code coverage
The plumb coverage command must check requirement coverage using string-matching
The plumb coverage command must print a formatted table using rich
The plumb status command must print a human-readable summary of tracked files, requirements, tests, and coverage
The skill file must be copied from plumb/skill/SKILL.md to .claude/SKILL.md during plumb init
The skill must be installed project-locally only, never globally
The system must support reading Claude Code native session files with JSONL parsing
Conversation logs must be located in session JSONL files using the format ~/.claude/projects/<project>/<session-uuid>.jsonl
The skill file must contain exact content as specified in the SKILL.md section
The plumb init command must append a specific Plumb status block to CLAUDE.md
The claude_log_path must be configurable in .plumb/config.json
If log path is not set, Plumb must attempt to auto-detect using common Claude Code log locations
If log is not found, Plumb must skip conversation analysis and continue with diff-only analysis
The log must be a JSONL file where each line contains role, content, and timestamp
Plumb must read only turns recorded after the last_commit timestamp
Chunking must be performed deterministically without LLM involvement
Primary chunking unit must be one user message plus all following assistant turns
Chunks exceeding 6,000 tokens must be split at tool call boundaries
Chunks must include one turn of overlap by prepending the final assistant turn of the previous chunk
Tool result turns longer than 500 tokens that appear to be file reads must be replaced with [file read: <filename>]
Multi-line assistant messages spanning multiple JSONL entries must be properly parsed and reassembled
The pre-commit hook must fire on amends and prevent duplicates by deleting decisions where commit_sha == last_commit
On every hook run, all stored SHAs must be checked against git history and unreachable SHAs flagged as broken
The decision log must be append-only with existing lines never modified in place
Status updates must be written as new lines with the same id
The latest line for a given id must be canonical
DiffAnalyzer must take raw unified diff string as input and output change summaries
DecisionExtractor must take chunk and diff_summary as input and output decision objects
QuestionSynthesizer must take a decision object with no question and output a plain-English question
RequirementParser must take markdown string as input and output requirement objects with text and ambiguous fields
SpecUpdater must take spec_section and approved decision object as input and output updated markdown
TestGenerator must take uncovered requirements, existing tests, and code context as input and output runnable pytest code
CodeModifier must use Claude API directly rather than DSPy for open-ended code modification
All CLI commands must fail gracefully with clear error messages if config.json is missing or malformed
All DSPy programs must retry on LLM failure with max 2 retries then raise PlumbInferenceError
The git hook must never exit non-zero due to internal Plumb errors
File writes must use temp file → rename to avoid partial writes
If plumb modify test run fails, Plumb must not stage the modification and update status to rejected_manual
The plumb status command must operate in cache-only mode to avoid making LLM calls
Session file reading must be optimized by pre-filtering based on file modification times
The system must achieve 80% test coverage minimum for v0.1.0
All CLI commands must run without error given valid inputs and update decisions.jsonl correctly
Decision log functions must implement read/write/filter/dedup on .jsonl with latest-line-wins logic
Git hook must produce correct pending decisions and handle amend detection and TTY vs non-TTY output
Conversation parser must handle correct chunk boundaries, overlap, noise reduction, and metadata
Each DSPy program must produce correctly structured output given fixture inputs
Coverage reporter must provide correct calculations given mock pytest output
Sync functionality must update spec and test files correctly given approved decisions with no partial writes
